title: Request Lines, Methods, Target Forms, Versions, and Routing Inputs · Go Semantics Case: Target Form Selection
summary: A parser uses Fields on the request line, normalizes targets before validation, treats every target as origin-form, and accepts invalid CONNECT authorities and absolute-form credentials. Planned artifact: Add and verify the target form selection increment in strict request-line and request-target parser. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish origin-form, absolute-form, authority-form, and asterisk-form using method and recipient role.
Recognize any valid method token, preserve case, then apply supported-method policy separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Target Form Selection · Theory Target Form Selection
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 5991 in a fuzzing harness varying every legal input segmentation. Build deterministic Go evidence to select the request-target form without normalizing away the raw routing boundary; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Target Form Selection contract for the supplied data.
